goal: Remove the redundant light 'second background' panel layer behind the reading result (#8) so the card+text sit directly on the page gradient
root_cause: The reading result wrapped its content in near-white .panel--spread / .panel--question-recap / #share-poster-root containers (rgba(255,255,255,0.9) + box-shadow), creating a page-gradient → white-panel → content stack. The white panel read as a needless '2nd bg' layer; the card art, title and lucky chip already have enough contrast on the lavender gradient.
fix: Appended §B2-8 to reading.css scoping body[data-page='reading'] #share-poster-root,.panel--spread,.panel--question-recap to background:transparent; box-shadow:none; border:none. Colour-only; layout/spacing untouched.
verified: Live-injected the same rule on the rendered daily result via chrome MCP and screenshotted before/after — card + 'YOUR CARD · TODAY' eyebrow + title + lucky chip float cleanly on the lavender gradient, no white frame. Confirmed share poster is unaffected (poster.js draws its own canvas, not this DOM). md5 parity canonical=www.
notes: Applies to all reading modes (shared .panel--spread); daily verified visually, question/celtic should be eyeballed on next QA but cards are self-contained so low risk.

goal: Clarify lifetime-stat label wording (#7) so each stat's unit is self-evident; keep the counting logic unchanged per user decision
fix: Per user ('keep units, change wording'): left counting untouched (cardsDrawn=cards so Celtic=10; questionsAsked=question sessions). Relabelled the ambiguous shares stat 'Readings shared'/'แชร์แล้ว' → 'Times shared'/'จำนวนแชร์' since it counts share actions, not distinct readings.
verified: node --check js/profile.js passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Stat labels are inline en/th in renderLifetimeStats (pre-existing pattern); edited in place rather than refactoring into common.js to stay surgical. Data-source caveat unchanged: cards/questions need login+history, shares are localStorage-local.

goal: Flush logged-out reading queue on session restore (INITIAL_SESSION), not only on fresh SIGNED_IN — close the #4/#5 robustness gap
root_cause: onAuthStateChange only called flushPendingReadings inside the `_event==='SIGNED_IN' && !prevUser` branch. Opening the app while already logged in emits INITIAL_SESSION (session restore), not SIGNED_IN, so a reading queued while logged-out (queuePendingReading) would sit in localStorage until the user did a *fresh* sign-in (sign out + back in) — potentially never. Made it look like history 'wasn't recording'.
fix: Split the handler: trackSigninSucceeded stays gated to fresh SIGNED_IN (no analytics on restore); flushPendingReadings now runs whenever session?.user exists on SIGNED_IN OR INITIAL_SESSION. flushPendingReadings is idempotent (removes the queue key before saving, daily-dedups via the 23505 path) so calling it on every restore is safe.
verified: DB diagnosis (Supabase SQL via Chrome agent) first confirmed recording itself WORKS — RLS INSERT policies correct, 32 readings/160 cards across 3 real Google users (kitikornr=26, another landing today); read_date always client-set, completed_at has a DB default, no triggers. So #4 was not a save failure but this restore-flush timing gap for logged-out draws. node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Also surfaced (no code change): Supabase auth SMTP is bouncing on test-signup aliases — irrelevant to Google/Apple OAuth users (no confirmation email sent), no fix needed for launch. Residual QA: on-device verify the offline→login flush bumps the row count.

goal: Fix the EN/TH toggle on the reading RESULT page — clicking TH left the page in English
root_cause: computeLanguageHref only rewrote a `lang` query param to the target locale, but the reading result page carries its locale in `l` (and initShell resolves currentLang via `lang` || `l`, where `l` wins). So toggling TH navigated to /th/reading.html but dragged the stale `l=en` along → the Thai mirror rendered English → toggle looked dead. (An in-place onLangToggle/switchLanguageInPlace path existed but goToLang never called it, and its body re-render didn't swap the card content anyway.)
fix: computeLanguageHref now rewrites BOTH `lang` and `l` params to the target locale, so navigating carries the reading's m/s/t/c params onto /th/reading.html with l=th — a fresh, fully-localized Thai render (the proven-correct path). goToLang reverted to navigation-only (dropped the dead in-place interception). All other pages unaffected (they have no `l` param).
verified: chrome MCP on localhost, cache-busted reload: /reading.html?…&l=en → click TH → /th/reading.html?…&l=th renders fully Thai (ไพ่ของคุณ·วันนี้, ไพ่กลับหัว, สำหรับวันนี้, Thai meaning, TH pill active, card preserved); reverse TH→EN → /reading.html?…&l=en. node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Card NAME still shows Thai (สามไม้เท้า) on the /th/ result heading — that's the existing /th/ data render, separate from this toggle fix and from the 'card names stay EN' interim direction; flag for a later parity pass if the EN-name rule should also apply to the TH result heading.
